marketState: neutral-to-bullish: extreme cooling demand and a crowded short COT offset by a storage surplus
summary: Front-month sits at $3.196, about $0.27 above the $2.929 fair-value estimate (SLIGHTLY OVER) but well inside the $2.429–$3.429 band, and the fair-value curve itself rises toward $3.087–$3.122 over the next 2-3 weeks so the market largely grows into today's price rather than needing a sharp correction. Weather demand is running far above normal — 1-7D and 8-16D demand are +31.7% and +20.3% vs the 5-year average and above the top of the historical range — during the seasonally favorable Cooling stretch where price already tracks above its typical seasonal path (+1.9pp vs median). Managed-money futures positioning is net short at just the 18th percentile of the past 5 years, a crowded-short setup that argues for upside skew if heat persists or storage disappoints. Working against that: the storage surplus widened to +175 Bcf vs the 5-year average with a bearish +13 Bcf surprise build, and daily/weekly technical trend readings lean bearish even though RSI (44-55) and Bollinger bands show no extreme. Net: the medium-term tape is modestly constructive (weather + seasonality + crowded short COT vs. a moderate storage overhang), while the very near-term picture is genuinely mixed and better suited to waiting for a cleaner level.

longTerm.rationale: Cooling demand is running +31.7% (1-7D) and +20.3% (8-16D) above the 5-year average and above the top of the historical range, during the seasonally strongest stretch (Sep is the strongest month) where price already sits above its typical seasonal path. Managed money is net short at only the 18th percentile of 5 years, a crowded-short setup, and the fair-value curve rises to meet today's price over the next 2-3 weeks rather than requiring price to fall.
longTerm.invalidation: Weather demand normalizes toward the 5-year average, storage surprises keep coming in bearish (builds > forecast, surplus widening beyond +175 Bcf), or price closes below the fair-value band support (~$2.75-2.80).

shortTerm.rationale: Price is only modestly rich to fair value (+$0.267, SLIGHTLY OVER but inside the band) while daily/weekly technical trend is bearish yet intraday momentum is mixed (MACD flips sign across timeframes, RSI neutral 44-55, price inside Bollinger bands on every timeframe, near-curve in only mild backwardation). There is no clean directional edge for a 1-10 day trade right now.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a pullback into the $2.95–$3.05 fair-value zone to go long, or a confirmed close below ~$3.10 with bearish MACD/EMA alignment across 1h/4h/1d to go short.

keyDrivers: Demand +31.7% (1-7D) / +20.3% (8-16D) vs 5y, both above the historical max range — strong cooling-season power burn
Managed money net short at the 18th percentile of the 5y range — crowded short, room to cover
Front $3.196 is SLIGHTLY OVER fair ($2.929, band $2.429-$3.429) but fair value rises to meet price over 3 weeks
Seasonality mildly bullish: 63% odds higher in 4 weeks, price already above its typical seasonal path
Storage surplus +175 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +13 Bcf surprise build caps upside
risks: Seasonality reliability is Low (51pp spread) — wide historical dispersion around the median move
